title: Sclerolaena × caput-casuarii

It is native to Australia, specifically found in the Northern Territory and Western Australia. Sclerolaena × caput-casuarii is often classified as an endemic species, meaning it is uniquely found in a specific geographic area. The classification of this species is primarily based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine, providing various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
